summary: On a Postgres schema exposed via PostgREST/Supabase to anon/authenticated, a privilege you did not explicitly close is open — deny is a thing you do, per object, and verify.

rules.rule: No SECURITY DEFINER function in an exposed schema may be executable by anon/authenticated unless a documented, audited allowlist entry derived from real client .rpc() call sites says it must be; control-plane functions are never on the allowlist.

watch.note: declared unwatched — no gate cites this canon yet; growth candidate

rules.rule: Revoke EXECUTE (and table/sequence privileges) from PUBLIC as well as anon/authenticated — revoking only the roles is a silent no-op — and verify the closure with has_*_privilege after revoking.

watch.note: declared unwatched — no gate cites this canon yet; growth candidate

rules.rule: No table in the exposed schema may lack RLS, and no FOR ALL/INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE policy with USING(true)/WITH CHECK(true) may be granted to anon — writes go server-side via service_role, authenticated tenant/row-scoped, or token-validated server-side.

watch.note: declared unwatched — no gate cites this canon yet; growth candidate

rules.rule: Pin search_path on every function (SECURITY INVOKER included, for determinism), and rely on the gate — not a one-time sweep — to catch functions added unpinned afterward.

watch.note: declared unwatched — no gate cites this canon yet; growth candidate

rules.rule: Run the Supabase Security Advisor as a required check at CI and/or pre-cutover: green means 0 security Errors and 0 security Warnings or a documented exception (rationale + owner) in the repo, and any new security-class warning fails the gate like a failing test.

watch.note: declared unwatched — no gate cites this canon yet; growth candidate

rules.rule: DB-security migrations are catalog-driven, idempotent, and end with an embedded self-test (DO $$ … RAISE EXCEPTION … $$) that re-checks the invariant and rolls back if the migration's own goal is not met.

watch.note: declared unwatched — no gate cites this canon yet; growth candidate

rules.rule: Install extensions in a dedicated extensions schema, not public; moving an existing extension is its OWN migration with a tested rollback, never bundled with other changes.

watch.note: declared unwatched — no gate cites this canon yet; growth candidate

rules.rule: Never leave a materialized view selectable by anon/authenticated (matviews ignore RLS — revoke and serve them server-side) nor a storage bucket publicly listable except by explicit intent; enable leaked-password protection.

watch.note: declared unwatched — no gate cites this canon yet; growth candidate
